_comment: M12 (Microsoft's CVC) Associate cut. M12 invests in AI, B2B SaaS, cloud infra, cybersecurity, DevOps, gaming/web3 — so this variant does NOT lead with the 'AI SaaS is un-investable / hard-tech' thesis (that argues against the fund). Instead it foregrounds frontier-AI fluency + B2B SaaS operating depth + sourcing/diligence + thesis-building & primary research (JD emphasizes building theses, opinionated views, unit economics + valuation modeling, IC memos, thought leadership). Built by /tailor-resume. Never fabricate.
name: Adam Breaux
summary: Investor-operator with three years across the venture lifecycle — sourcing and diligencing deals for a $100M+ foundation VC arm, building four B2B SaaS companies from 0 to 1, and running fund operations and LP reporting on an $8M fund. Pairs deep financial-modeling and diligence judgment with rare, hands-on frontier-AI fluency: nicknamed 'AdamGPT' by leadership in 2022 and a builder of production multi-agent systems for deal sourcing and operations. Builds opinionated, research-backed theses, including ~100 primary founder interviews mapping a sector from the ground up.

items.title: Associate — Chief of Staff to CEO / Venture Operations Lead
bullets: 0-to-1 venture lead: Took Agentech from a blank page to a funded B2B SaaS company (~$25M valuation today) — thesis through pivot to AI-powered insurance-claims servicing; built the team and ran sales to the first $600K ARR. Now its part-time CFO and Chief of Staff.

Diligence & financial modeling: Built three-statement, returns, and unit-economics models and ran competitive analysis across 500+ companies; computed fund TVPI for fundraising. Owned Agentech's $3M seed end to end — investor memo, financial model, projections, and data room — to a fast, fully-subscribed close, and ran the same process across multiple ventures (~$10M raised studio-wide).

Fund operations (built from scratch): For an $8M fund, defined the venture- and studio-level metrics and KPIs that mattered, stood up portfolio tracking, reporting, and portfolio-review presentations, and became the direct point of contact LPs come to for updates; led the annual LP meeting end to end.

Production AI systems: Built a multi-agent investor-sourcing engine with a continual-learning loop (ranked by geography, thesis, and stage; enriched via Clay) that produced a pipeline of hundreds of investors, plus staged-pipeline and term-sheet-evaluation tooling; and a Claude-based operations platform spanning CRM, call transcripts, email, calendar, and contracts that auto-drafts morning briefs, meeting agendas, and proposals.

items.title: Venture Fellow / Investment Diligence
items.org: Atento Capital  —  VC arm of the George Kaiser Family Foundation ($100M+ fund)
bullets: Sourced 14 of 20 finalists and 4 of 7 startups selected to pitch — the most of any of ~12 fellows; screened hundreds of companies against the fund's themes.
Ran screening calls, diligenced founding teams, assessed commercial viability, and presented investment theses to internal committees.
Recalled by principals to support diligence on two Core Fund I deals: bottom-up market sizing, competitive analysis, and financial-projection review delivered directly to a GP.
Judgment, validated: On all three deals I diligenced, I recommended against — on scalability, market size, and an unconvincing expansion story; all three are no longer operating.

items.title: Co-Founder — Aeon (bespoke enterprise-AI products)
bullets: Co-founded a practice that builds bespoke AI products for mid-market companies; own executive discovery, problem scoping, and adoption — primary-source insight into how frontier AI is actually deployed in production and where it creates durable value.
Built an investment thesis from primary research — ~100 founder and operator interviews mapping a sector's structure, emerging trends, and where value accrues in an AI-saturated market.

items.title: Founding President — starTUp (University of Tulsa VC Club)
bullets: Founded TU's first VC club and placed members as analysts on the 46VC Hurricane Venture Fund, a multi-million-dollar student/alumni fund.

items.title: Chair, Macroeconomic Committee — TU Student Investment Fund ($8M)
bullets: Implemented an ETF strategy and decision process still in use today; made buy/sell calls on macro trends and led analysts to execute.

items.title: Master of Business Administration — Finance & Entrepreneurship
B.S. Exercise Science / Pre-Med
items.org: The University of Tulsa  —  Division I Cross Country & Track and Field

sections.heading: Skills & Strengths
